title: Welcome to Claw3D
description: A guided tour through Claw3D — the 3D workspace and visualization layer for AI agents powered by OpenClaw. Explore the real architecture: Next.js frontend, Phaser-powered 3D office, agent management, gateway proxy, and more.
prerequisites: Basic understanding of AI agents and automation
Familiarity with Next.js, React, and TypeScript

steps.title: Welcome to Claw3D
steps.description:
Claw3D is an open-source 3D workspace where AI agents collaborate visually. Agents review code, run tests, and execute tasks inside a shared 3D office environment — think of it as a virtual office for your AI team.

The project is a Next.js application with a Phaser-based 3D rendering engine, a WebSocket gateway proxy, and a rich agent management system. OpenClaw provides the intelligence underneath.

interactiveActions.description: Click on README.md to see the full project overview

steps.title: Next.js Application Shell
steps.description:
The app is built on Next.js with the App Router. The main entry points are:

• page.tsx — Home screen and landing page
• layout.tsx — Root layout wrapping the entire app
• office/ — The 3D office workspace route
• agents/ — Agent fleet management and settings
• api/ — Server-side API routes for gateway, office, and studio

The [..invalid] catch-all route handles unknown paths gracefully.

interactiveActions.description: Click on page.tsx to see the home screen

steps.title: Agent Management System
steps.description:
The agent system is the heart of Claw3D. It handles the full lifecycle of AI agents:

• components/ — UI for agent chat, creation, inspection, skills, and fleet sidebar
• approvals/ — Execution approval workflows with control loops and pause policies
• operations/ — Agent runtime operations
• state/ — Agent state management
• hooks/ — React hooks for agent interactions
• creation/ — Agent provisioning and setup

Agents communicate through the OpenClaw Gateway, with approvals ensuring human oversight of agent actions.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over the approvals directory to see the execution approval workflows

steps.title: The 3D Office
steps.description:
The visual centerpiece: a Phaser-powered 3D office where agents sit at desks, collaborate, and work. The office system includes:

• phaser/ — Rendering engine with scenes, objects, overlays, and navigation systems
• components/ — Office UI panels: analytics, inbox, history, playbooks, skills marketplace, and settings
• hooks/ — Office-specific logic like standup controllers and usage analytics
• retro-office/ — The retro-themed 3D variant with furniture geometry, desk navigation routes, and persistence

3D furniture models (desks, chairs, computers, plants) are stored as GLB files in public/office-assets/.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over the phaser directory to see the 3D rendering system

steps.title: Gateway Proxy & Server
steps.description:
The browser never connects directly to the OpenClaw Gateway. Everything flows through the Studio proxy for security:

• server/ — Express server with gateway-proxy.js (WebSocket forwarding), access-gate.js (auth), network-policy.js (security), and studio-settings.js (local config)
• src/lib/gateway/ — Client-side gateway abstraction with GatewayClient.ts, agent config, exec approvals, and error handling
• src/app/api/gateway/ — Next.js API routes for agent-state, media, and skills

This layered proxy design keeps credentials server-side and allows flexible deployment.

interactiveActions.description: Click on gateway-proxy.js to see the WebSocket proxy

steps.title: Shared Libraries & Utilities
steps.description:
The src/lib/ directory contains shared infrastructure used across the app:

• office/ — Office state management, desk directives, presence, standup system, and event triggers
• gateway/ — Gateway client and OpenClaw integration
• agents/ — Avatar management
• security/ — Permission sandboxing (see docs/permissions-sandboxing.md)
• skills/ — Skill system integration
• cron/ — Scheduled task support
• ssh/, dom/, text/, uuid.ts — Low-level utilities

This is the connective tissue that links the features together.

interactiveActions.description: Hover over the office library to see state management and event systems

steps.title: Testing & Developer Tooling
steps.description:
Claw3D uses a multi-layer testing strategy:

• tests/unit/ — Unit tests with Vitest
• tests/e2e/ — End-to-end tests with Playwright
• vitest.config.ts & playwright.config.ts — Test configuration
• scripts/ — Developer scripts for cleanup, smoke testing, and OpenClaw gateway sync
• eslint.config.mjs — Linting rules
• .env.example — Environment variable template for local setup

Before contributing: run lint, typecheck, and tests. Keep PRs focused — one topic per PR.

interactiveActions.description: Click on .env.example to see the required environment variables

steps.title: What's Next
steps.description:
You've explored the full Claw3D codebase! Here's where to go from here:

• README.md — Quick start guide and project overview
• ARCHITECTURE.md — System boundaries and data flow
• ROADMAP.md — Current priorities and good first contributions
• CONTRIBUTING.md — Local setup and PR expectations
• CODE_DOCUMENTATION.md — Detailed code-level documentation
• TUTORIAL.md — Step-by-step walkthrough

The project is early-stage and actively evolving. Check GitHub Issues for current work. Welcome to the team!

interactiveActions.description: Explore the full codebase on your own
